Class JavaSourceElement

java.lang.Object
recoder.java.JavaSourceElement
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Cloneable, SourceElement
Direct Known Subclasses:
Comment, JavaProgramElement
public abstract class JavaSourceElement extends Object implements SourceElement, Cloneable, Serializable
Top level implementation of a Java SourceElement. This class already knows its ProgramFactory.
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

    Creates a syntactical representation of the source element using the SourceElement.accept(SourceVisitor)method with an internal default pretty printer.

  • Constructor Details

    • JavaSourceElement

      public JavaSourceElement()
      Java source element.

    • JavaSourceElement

      protected JavaSourceElement(JavaSourceElement proto)
      Java source element.
      Parameters:
      proto - a java source element.

  • Method Details

    • getFirstElement

      public SourceElement getFirstElement()
      Finds the source element that occurs first in the source. The default implementation returns this element, which is correct for all terminal program elements, and many non terminals such as statements and prefixed operators.
      Specified by:
      getFirstElement in interface SourceElement
      Returns:
      the first source element in the syntactical representation of this element, may be equals to this element.
      See Also:
      • toSource()
      • getStartPosition()

    • getLastElement

      public SourceElement getLastElement()
      Finds the source element that occurs last in the source. The default implementation returns this element, which is correct for all terminal program elements, and many non terminals such as statements and prefixed operators.
      Specified by:
      getLastElement in interface SourceElement
      Returns:
      the last source element in the syntactical representation of this element, may be equals to this element.
      See Also:
      • toSource()
      • getEndPosition()

    • getStartPosition

      public final SourceElement.Position getStartPosition()
      Returns the start position of the primary token of this element. To get the start position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getFirstElement().
      Specified by:
      getStartPosition in interface SourceElement
      Returns:
      the start position of the primary token.

    • setStartPosition

      public final void setStartPosition(SourceElement.Position p)
      Sets the start position of the primary token of this element. To set the start position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getFirstElement().
      Specified by:
      setStartPosition in interface SourceElement
      Parameters:
      p - the start position of the primary token.

    • getEndPosition

      public final SourceElement.Position getEndPosition()
      Returns the end position of the primary token of this element. To get the end position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getLastElement().
      Specified by:
      getEndPosition in interface SourceElement
      Returns:
      the end position of the primary token.

    • setEndPosition

      public final void setEndPosition(SourceElement.Position p)
      Sets the end position of the primary token of this element. To set the end position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getLastElement().
      Specified by:
      setEndPosition in interface SourceElement
      Parameters:
      p - the end position of the primary token.

    • getRelativePosition

      public final SourceElement.Position getRelativePosition()
      Returns the relative position (number of blank heading lines and columns) of the primary token of this element. To get the relative position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getFirstElement().
      Specified by:
      getRelativePosition in interface SourceElement
      Returns:
      the relative position of the primary token.

    • setRelativePosition

      public final void setRelativePosition(SourceElement.Position p)
      Sets the relative position (number of blank heading lines and columns) of the primary token of this element. To set the relative position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getFirstElement().
      Specified by:
      setRelativePosition in interface SourceElement
      Parameters:
      p - the relative position of the primary token.

    • getFactory

      public final ProgramFactory getFactory()
      Get factory.
      Specified by:
      getFactory in interface SourceElement
      Returns:
      the program factory.

    • toSource

      public String toSource()
      Description copied from interface: SourceElement
      Creates a syntactical representation of the source element using the SourceElement.accept(SourceVisitor)method with an internal default pretty printer.
      Specified by:
      toSource in interface SourceElement

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
